channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Biden is receiving radiation and hormone therapy to treat his prostate cancer
item.description: Former President Joe Biden's treatment for an aggressive form of prostate cancer has entered a new phase.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: As the National Guard enters Memphis, memories of MLK and 1968 unrest resurface
item.description: As the National Guard enters Memphis, some longtime residents are recalling the thousands sent there in 1968.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Mattise And Adrian Sainz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: OSHA violations reported prior to massive explosion at Tennessee facility: documents
item.description: As investigators continue to comb through the wreckage of an explosion in Tennessee that left 18 people unaccounted for Friday, Nexstar's WKRN has learned more about the company that operated the facility.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Wilson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pentagon will build a training facility for Qatari pilots in Idaho
item.description: The U.S. military is moving forward with plans to build a dedicated facility in Idaho to train pilots from Qatar, an important U.S. ally in the Middle East, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Thousands of Palestinians return to what's left of their homes as Gaza ceasefire takes effect
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — Tens of thousands of Palestinians headed back to the heavily destroyed northern Gaza Strip on Friday as a U.S.-brokered ceasefire came into effect in a deal that raised hopes for ending the Israel-Hamas war. All the remaining hostages were set to be released within days.
item.dc:creator: ABDEL KAREEM HANA, WAFAA SHURAFA and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Nor'easter without a name to bring ocean flooding and high winds to US East Coast
item.description: A storm without a name was expected to bring flooding to the East Coast from South Carolina to New Jersey and high winds to New York City over the holiday weekend.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump threatens tech export limits, new 100% tariff on Chinese imports starting Nov. 1 or sooner
item.description: Trump said that “starting November 1st, 2025 (or sooner, depending on any further actions or changes taken by China), the United States of America will impose a Tariff of 100% on China, over and above any Tariff that they are currently paying.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: Missing mom found alive in forest as officials investigate ‘suspicious’ house fire
item.description: Britney Gard, 46, called 911 from a forest near Bainbridge, Indiana, ending a week-long search that began after her house caught fire under suspicious circumstances.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Peru wakes up to 7th president in less than a decade after President Boluarte ousted by Congress
item.description: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Peruvians woke up to their seventh president in less than a decade Friday, as 38-year-old José Jerí took the reins of the shaken nation after Congress ousted widely loathed President Dina Boluarte in a lightening overnight impeachment.
item.dc:creator: FRANKLIN BRICEÑO, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Family of man who died on Stardust Racers demands its closure after new reports of malfunction
item.description: The family of Kevin Rodriguez Zavala, the Epic Universe park visitor who died after riding the Stardust Racers roller coaster, issued a statement demanding that the ride be closed after new reports of restraint malfunctions.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, NewsNation

item.title: GOP senators urge RFK Jr. to crack down on medication abortion
item.description: Almost every Senate Republican on Thursday urged the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to “reevaluate” whether a newly approved generic version of the abortion drug mifepristone should stay on the market. The letter to Health and Human Services (HHS) Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Watch: House Republicans give remarks on shutdown Day 10
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) and House Republican leaders gave remarks Friday morning as the government shutdown stretched into a 10th day.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Drake's defamation lawsuit against label over Kendrick Lamar's 'Not Like Us' tossed out
item.description: In his lawsuit Drake asserted that the song amounts to “falsely accusing him of being a sex offender, engaging in pedophilic acts” and more.
item.dc:creator: Larry Newumeister, ABC 4

item.title: Tennessee teacher fired over Charlie Kirk posts files free speech lawsuit
item.description: A longtime Rutherford County teacher is suing her school district, claiming she was fired for what she shared online.
item.dc:creator: Kelly Milan, Fox 5

item.title: US is sending about 200 troops to Israel to help support and monitor the Gaza ceasefire deal
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The United States is sending about 200 troops to Israel to help support and monitor the ceasefire deal in Gaza as part of a team that includes partner nations, nongovernmental organizations and private-sector players, U.S. officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pro-Palestinian agitators hit with felony 'promotion of anarchy' charges after riot in Dem-run city: officials
item.description: Agitators arrested in Tuesday night's pro-Palestinian riot now face a felony “promotion of anarchy" charge, according to the district attorney's office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Most states with National Guard troops in DC plan to withdraw this fall
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than half the states contributing National Guard troops to President Donald Trump's federal law enforcement initiative in Washington have set target dates for their withdrawal later this fall, state officials told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Rhode Island dentist helps save peregrine falcon with broken beak
item.description: "The keratin from the base that's damaged is going to grow back," Sheida Soleimani said. "So we did a kind of bridge with dental epoxy.
item.dc:creator: Raymond Baccari, NewsNation

item.title: Travelers continue to endure delays amid government shutdown
item.description: On Thursday, Duffy told Fox Business that he may fire air traffic controllers who haven't shown up for work.
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: Taco Bell, KFC franchisee using AI to improve customer experience
item.description: Rather than displacing employees, as some fear AI might, Coach AI can serve as "a tool to help the worker.
item.dc:creator: Zach Kaplan, NewsNation

item.title: CDC panel to review childhood vaccine schedules
item.description: The vaccine advisory panel for the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) is planning to look into the timing of other childhood vaccine schedules, soon after it voted to delay the measles, mumps, rubella and chickenpox vaccine.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KRON4

item.title: Arizona sheriff's office misused millions meant to remedy racial profiling, report reveals
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — The sheriff’s office for metro Phoenix spent millions of dollars budgeted for compliance costs in a racial profiling case over Joe Arpaio’s immigration crackdowns on things that had little or nothing to do with a court-ordered overhaul of the agency, according to an expert’s report.
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tampa Bay lawmaker formally nominates Trump for Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-Fla.), in a letter to members of the Norwegian Nobel Committee, said she felt Trump deserved the prize “in recognition of his bold and unprecedented leadership in advancing peace around the globe.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Political Power Ranker: Analyzing this year's biggest elections
item.description: On this week's edition of Political Power Ranker, we're breaking down some of the biggest races across the country.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, Fox 5

item.title: Top DHS official defends ICE agents filmed shooting pepper ball at pastor's head
item.description: (The Hill) – Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Assistant Secretary Tricia McLaughlin on Wednesday defended an Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officer who shot a pastor with a pepper ball from a rooftop after video of the incident went viral this week. Videos of the Sept.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Antifa expert at Rutgers University says he is moving to Spain because of death threats
item.description: An expert on anti-fascist groups who teaches at Rutgers University says he is fleeing with his family to Spain after getting death threats amid a campaign by the campus chapter of Turning Point USA and other conservative groups to get him fired.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Oregon general's comments raise eyebrows a week later
item.description: In the clip, Brigadier General Alan Gronewold said Oregon National Guard members “will be protecting any protesters at the ICE facility.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Israel and Hamas agree to a ceasefire, release of hostages and prisoners
item.description: Israel and Hamas have agreed to a pause in their devastating two-year war and the release of the remaining hostages in exchange for Palestinian prisoners.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: New Jersey businessman who testified against ex-Sen. Bob Menendez won't go to prison
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A New Jersey businessman who testified against former Democratic Sen. Bob Menendez and his wife at separate bribery trials won’t go to prison, after a judge credited him at his sentencing Thursday for showing honesty on the witness stand and sincere remorse.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump expects Israeli hostages to be released on Monday
item.description: President Donald Trump says he expects the remaining hostages to be released from Gaza on Monday as part of the first phase of Trump's 20-point peace plan.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: St. Petersburg to vote on flags for LGBTQ+ and Black communities after DeSantis erases murals
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — The Florida city of St. Petersburg, long a bastion of LGBTQ+ rights and mindful of its racially segregated past, is considering approval of two flags recognizing those issues after Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kiss rocker Gene Simmons recovering after crashing SUV
item.description: MALIBU, Calif. (AP) — Kiss rock star Gene Simmons is recovering after he fainted while driving along the Pacific Coast Highway in Malibu and crashed his SUV into a parked car, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Flight delayed or canceled during government shutdown? Here's what you should do
item.description: The federal government shutdown has entered its second week, and already shortages of air traffic controllers have strained operations and disrupted flights at some U.S. airports.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK and RIO YAMAT AP Transportation and Airlines Writers, Fox 5

item.title: VIDEO: Monarch butterfly with broken wing gets wing transplant
item.description: A monarch butterfly with a broken wing was miraculously able to fly again after it underwent a wing transplant at a nature preserve.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nobel Prize in literature awarded to Hungarian writer László Krasznahorkai
item.description: Hungarian writer László Krasznahorkai, whose philosophical, bleakly funny novels often unfold in single sentences, won the Nobel Prize in literature Thursday for his for his "compelling and visionary oeuvre that, in the midst of apocalyptic terror, reaffirms the power of art.
item.dc:creator: KOSTYA MANENKOV and MIKE CORDER Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump was nominated for the Nobel Peace Prize before. Experts say he's unlikely to win this year
item.description: STAVANGER, Norway (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump’s bid to win the Nobel Peace Prize has drawn added attention to the annual guessing game over who its next laureate will be.
item.dc:creator: MARK LEWIS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Democrats win momentum over GOP in shutdown fight
item.description: As the shutdown fight stretches into its second week, the winds have shifted in favor of the Democrats — at least for the moment. Early polls say voters are more likely to blame President Trump and the Republicans for the lengthy impasse.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC
